commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an opt-in Slack `streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards` mode that renders progress draft lines as native Slack plan/task stream chunks, with schema, docs, metadata, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR adds an opt-in Slack feature. The relevant verification is current-head real Slack behavior proof plus focused dispatch/schema tests.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor-provided current-head Slack proof and maintainer acceptance of the opted-in delivery limitations, not an automated repair PR.

target.title: Render Slack progress drafts as plan cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Add Slack native progress task cards as an explicit opt-in feature for `channels.slack.streaming.mode="progress"` via `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`.
- Preserve existing progress-mode behavior by keeping the portable draft-preview path when the new opt-in is unset.
- Keep Slack native progress plan/task rendering and completion updates for opted-in accounts, including first running-tool visibility before final text.
- Remove out-of-scope tracker/proof-helper and SDK identity changes from this PR.

## Verification
- `git diff --check HEAD~2..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-dispatch-streaming-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts` (26 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-dispatch-preview-rerun-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` (44 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-config-schema-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts` (39 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-slack-progress-blocks-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts` (19 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-slack-streaming-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` (19 passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress task cards are now opt-in through `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`; existing Slack progress mode remains on the portable draft-preview path unless that flag is set.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch at commit `12e07b2fa1`, Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, infra-engineer-originated message mentioning Baby Jules. For this proof only, the source-dev Slack config was temporarily set to `streaming.mode="progress"` and `streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`, then restored from backup after the run.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the local source-run gateway with Baby Jules Slack app credentials, verified `/healthz` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` and logs showed `slack socket mode connected`; ran ingestion smoke marker `SMOKE-BABY-OPTIN-20260519T211934Z`; then ran `MARKER=SEQ34-R12-OPTIN-20260519T212022Z ARTIFACT=/tmp/openclaw-SEQ34-R12-OPTIN-20260519T212022Z-slack-transient-e2e.json python3 /tmp/openclaw-slack-transient-e2e.py`.
Evidence after fix: Slack thread `https://4okworkspace.slack.com/archives/C0ASNARACMT/p1779225624187979`; artifact `/tmp/openclaw-SEQ34-R12-OPTIN-20260519T212022Z-slack-transient-e2e.json`; pass flags were `pre_final_native_task_visible=true`, `final_text_seen=true`, and `final_native_task_complete=true`. A pre-final Slack API snapshot at `2026-05-19T21:20:40.773803Z` showed a native `plan` block with task status `in_progress` before the final `FINAL-SEQ34-R12-OPTIN-20260519T212022Z` reply.
Observed result after fix: Baby Jules consumed the infra-engineer prompt, posted a Slack-native task card while the command was still running, then updated the task to complete and posted the exact final marker reply. Source-dev config was restored and the live gateway process was stopped after proof.
What was not tested: screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; proof is Slack Web API thread snapshots plus gateway health/socket logs. Repeated same-name tool calls without upstream stable IDs may still collapse, and a failed command followed by a normal final assistant reply may finalize the native task as complete rather than error; both are intentionally left out of scope to keep this PR limited to the opt-in Slack-native progress feature.
target.author: simonvanlaak
